title: HTML5 Form Validation Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the advanced attributes and methods for validating forms in HTML5.

questions.question: Which attribute can you add to a <form> tag to disable the browser's built-in validation?
The JavaScript property available on every input that contains information about its validation state is named what?
Which method should you use on an input element to set a custom error message?
Which CSS pseudo-class should be used to style an element differently when it fails the browser's validation requirements?
If you call setCustomValidity('Error message'), will the input be considered valid or invalid?
options: Valid, but with a warning message.
Invalid, as long as the string provided is not empty.
It depends on the the user's browser type.
It doesn't affect the input's actual validity state.
